title: Boat Rental in Oslo Fjord: A Cost and Season Guide for 2026
html:
Exploring Oslo Fjord by boat in 2026 offers a unique perspective on Norway's stunning coastline, with prices for a half-day motorboat hire starting from around €250. Whether you depart from Aker Brygge or Vollen, using a platform like BoatHire24 makes finding the right vessel straightforward.

Where to set off in Oslo Fjord

Start your journey from Aker Brygge, a lively harbour in central Oslo, or head to Vollen Marina for a quieter embarkation. Other popular departure points include Hankø Island, known for its summer regattas, and the small town of Drøbak, which offers sheltered moorings. For longer trips, consider setting sail from Nesodden or the yacht club at Fornebu. Each of these locations provides easy access to the open waters of the fjord and to islands like Hovedøya and Langøyene.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motorboat, best for a fast trip to nearby islands, up to 6 guests, from €250 for a half-day.
  2. Sailing yacht, ideal for a relaxed cruise, up to 8 guests, from €600 for a full day.
  3. Catamaran, spacious and stable for groups, up to 12 guests, from €900 for a full day.
  4. Rigid inflatable boat (RIB), perfect for thrill-seekers, up to 4 guests, from €200 for a two-hour ride.
  5. Crewed luxury yacht, offers full service and comfort, up to 10 guests, from €1,500 for a day charter.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Oslo Fjord?

A half-day self-drive motorboat typically costs between €250 and €400, while a full-day luxury yacht charter can reach €2,000 or more. Prices vary by vessel size, season, and whether crew is included. You can compare verified boat listings for yacht charter Oslo Fjord to find the best value. Shared tour options, such as a two-hour sightseeing RIB ride, start from around €80 per person. For a full day on a catamaran for up to 12 guests, expect to pay from €900 to €1,500. A crewed motor yacht with skipper and host may cost €1,500 to €3,000 per day.

  • Shared RIB tour: from €80 per person for a two-hour trip
  • Small motorboat half-day: €250, €400 for up to 6 guests
  • Catamaran full-day: €900, €1,500 for up to 12 guests
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day: €1,500, €3,000 for up to 10 guests

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Oslo Fjord?

In Norway, a boat licence is generally not required for small motorboats under 15 horsepower, but larger craft may require a certificate. The operator will confirm exact requirements during booking. For sailing yachts and larger motorboats, a valid ICC (International Certificate of Competence) or equivalent is often needed. Always check with the hire company to avoid any issues.

If you plan to anchor for lunch, try the sheltered bay of Oksval on Nesodden; it is a local favourite for calm swimming stops.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Oslo Fjord?

The peak season runs from June to August, when the weather is warmest and the fjord is bustling with activity. The prevailing southwesterly wind can build during the afternoon, so morning trips are often calmer. September offers quieter conditions with still pleasant temperatures. Avoid April and October unless you are comfortable with cooler weather.

At a glance

  • Price band: €250, €3,000 per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Typical trip length: half-day to full day (4, 8 hours)
  • Guest capacity: 2, 12 guests depending on vessel
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence: Required for larger motorboats and sailing yachts

BoatHire24 makes it simple to find and rent a boat in Oslo Fjord. For example, the Fairline Targa 43 offers a refined cruising experience and you can view the Fairline listing for full specifications. Whether you choose a nimble Axopar or a spacious Lagoon catamaran, the fjord’s sheltered waters and forested islands promise an unforgettable day afloat.

Questions about boat rental in Oslo Fjord

Can I rent a boat in Oslo Fjord without a licence?

Yes, for small motorboats under 15 hp no licence is needed, but for larger vessels you should confirm with the operator.

What is the best way to reach the islands in Oslo Fjord?

Renting a motorboat from Aker Brygge or Vollen gives you the freedom to explore islands like Hovedøya at your own pace.

How far is it from Oslo to Drøbak by boat?

The direct route from Aker Brygge to Drøbak is about 18 nautical miles, a journey of roughly an hour at cruising speed.

What should I pack for a day on the fjord?

Bring layers, sun protection, snacks, and plenty of water, as there are few shops on the outer islands.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Oslo Fjord and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
